channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kisii Family Holds Joint Burial for Mother and Son After They Die Hours Apart Following Road Accident
item.description: A family in Kisii County has been left grieving after losing a mother and her son within hours of each other before laying them to rest in a joint burial ceremony. According to the report, the son, id...

item.title: Franklin Drilon calls for efficient management of Sara Duterte's impeachment trial amid public fatigue
item.description: Former Senate president Franklin Drilon has urged the Senate impeachment court to expedite the trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, citing growing public fatigue with the prolonged proceedings. Durin...

item.title: World Cup final: Mixed reactions to halftime show as Spain edges Argentina in thrilling extra-time clash
item.description: The FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina ended in dramatic fashion, with Spain securing a 1-0 victory in extra time after a tense and emotional match. Lionel Messi's Argentina suffered a h...

item.title: Khampepe Commission Continues Hearings on TRC Cases
item.description: The Khampepe commission of inquiry, tasked with examining alleged efforts to delay the prosecution of apartheid-era perpetrators, is holding hearings in Newtown, Johannesburg. The commission's work fo...

item.title: Labor Appoints Ex-Liberal Premier to Promote Multiculturalism Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Former Tasmanian Liberal premier Peter Gutwein has been appointed by the Labor Party as chair of Australia’s peak multicultural advisory council. The move comes amid heated debates on the political ri...

item.title: Eskom Reassures Public No Radiation Leak at Koeberg Unit 2 During Maintenance
item.description: Eskom has confirmed that no radiation leak occurred during routine maintenance at Koeberg Nuclear Power Station's Unit 2, assuring the public there was no risk to employees, communities, or the enviro...

item.title: Huaxin's Expansion in Nigerian Cement Market Challenges Local Duopoly
item.description: China’s Huaxin Group has rebranded Lafarge Africa as HBM Nigeria, signaling its aggressive expansion plans in the country’s cement industry. The $1bn acquisition of Holcim’s stake in Lafarge Africa la...

item.title: Ryan Greer to Lead Marist College Canberra from Next Year

item.title: The Hidden Role of Associations in Shaping Canadian Society

item.title: Tragedy in Khyber: Flash Flood Claims Lives of Afghan Families
item.description: A flash flood triggered by heavy rains swept away a vehicle carrying Afghan families returning to their homeland in the Khyber district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on Monday. Rescue 1122 confirmed the recov...

item.title: CentreBC Appoints Mike Bernier as New Party Leader
item.description: Vancouver CentreBC has appointed Mike Bernier, a former cabinet minister, as its new party leader. This decision comes as the party seeks to strengthen its position in the political landscape. Bernier...

item.title: Retired Bishop Warns of Political Crisis and Calls for Release of Opposition Leader
item.description: Bishop Dr Glorius Shoo, a retired religious leader from Tanzania, has warned that the nation is facing a profound crisis of hope driven by political deception and exclusion. In an exclusive interview ...
